words.definitionEn: something that exists separately from other things and has its own identity
words.exampleEn: The unit has become part of a larger department and no longer exists as a separate entity.

words.definitionEn: to state something as a fact but without giving proof
words.exampleEn: allege (that)…, The prosecution alleges (that) she was driving carelessly.

words.definitionEn: a large number of cases of a particular disease or medical condition happening at the same time in a particular community
words.exampleEn: the outbreak of a flu epidemic

words.definitionEn: the fact of being equal in rights, status, advantages, etc.
words.exampleEn: racial/social/gender equality

words.definitionEn: a statement showing that two amounts or values are equal, for example 2x + y = 54
words.exampleEn: the numbers on the right-hand side of the equation

words.definitionEn: expressed as though something is a fact but without giving any proof
words.exampleEn: crimes allegedly committed during the war

words.definitionEn: to build something
words.exampleEn: The church was erected in 1582.

words.definitionEn: to become greater, worse, more serious, etc.; to make something greater, worse, more serious, etc.
words.exampleEn: the escalating costs of healthcare

words.definitionEn: an agreement between countries, political parties, etc. to work together in order to achieve something that they all want
words.exampleEn: to form/make an alliance

words.definitionEn: the most important quality or feature of something, that makes it what it is
words.exampleEn: His paintings capture the essence of France.

words.definitionEn: an organization, a large institution or a hotel
words.exampleEn: The visa is for foreign nationals wishing to study at a university, college or similar educational establishment.

words.definitionEn: to give something officially to somebody/something for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: allocate something (for something), A large sum has been allocated for buying new books for the library.

words.definitionEn: without an end; existing or continuing forever
words.exampleEn: the promise of eternal life in heaven

words.definitionEn: to move people from a place of danger to a safer place
words.exampleEn: evacuate something, Police evacuated nearby buildings.

words.definitionEn: an amount of money, space, etc. that is given to somebody for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: We have spent our entire allocation for the year.

words.definitionEn: to bring a feeling, a memory or an image into your mind
words.exampleEn: The music evoked memories of her youth.

words.definitionEn: connected with evolution; connected with slow steady development and change
words.exampleEn: evolutionary theory

words.definitionEn: an amount of money that is given to somebody regularly or for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: an allowance of $20 a day

words.definitionEn: to make something seem larger, better, worse or more important than it really is
words.exampleEn: The hotel was really filthy and I'm not exaggerating.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being extremely good
words.exampleEn: a reputation for academic excellence

words.definitionEn: the use of something in a way that is wrong or harmful
words.exampleEn: alcohol/drug abuse

words.definitionEn: a country that has agreed to help and support another country, especially in case of a war
words.exampleEn: our European/NATO allies

words.definitionEn: unusually good
words.exampleEn: At the age of five he showed exceptional talent as a musician.

words.definitionEn: in addition to an amount that is necessary, usual or legal
words.exampleEn: Excess food is stored as fat.

words.definitionEn: more than is necessary, reasonable or acceptable
words.exampleEn: You can throw away any excess.

words.definitionEn: the act of preventing somebody/something from entering a place or taking part in something
words.exampleEn: exclusion (of somebody/something) (from something), He was disappointed with his exclusion from the England squad.

words.definitionEn: only to be used by one particular person or group; only given to one particular person or group
words.exampleEn: The hotel has exclusive access to the beach.

words.definitionEn: for only one particular person, group or use
words.exampleEn: The resort caters almost exclusively for a high-society public.

words.definitionEn: to kill somebody, especially as a legal punishment
words.exampleEn: be executed (for something), He was executed for treason.

words.definitionEn: the act of killing somebody, especially as a legal punishment
words.exampleEn: He faced execution by hanging for murder.

words.definitionEn: to use power or influence to affect somebody/something
words.exampleEn: He exerted all his authority to make them accept the plan.

words.definitionEn: the state of being sent to live in another country that is not your own, especially for political reasons or as a punishment
words.exampleEn: a place of exile

words.definitionEn: to go out; to leave a building, stage, vehicle, etc.
words.exampleEn: (+ adv./prep.), The bullet entered her back and exited through her chest.

words.definitionEn: the act of spending or using money; an amount of money spent
words.exampleEn: a reduction in public/government/military expenditure

words.definitionEn: based on new ideas, forms or methods that are used to find out what effect they have
words.exampleEn: The school's experimental teaching methods include letting the children decide what to study.

words.definitionEn: to be no longer legally acceptable because the period of time for which it could be used has ended
words.exampleEn: When does your driving licence expire?

words.definitionEn: clear and easy to understand, so that you have no doubt what is meant
words.exampleEn: He gave me very explicit directions on how to get there.

words.definitionEn: clearly or directly, so that the meaning is easy to understand
words.exampleEn: The report states explicitly that the system was to blame.

words.definitionEn: a situation in which somebody treats somebody else in an unfair way, especially in order to make money from their work
words.exampleEn: the exploitation of children

words.definitionEn: easily able or likely to explode
words.exampleEn: an explosive device (= a bomb)

words.definitionEn: a substance that is able or likely to cause an explosion
words.exampleEn: plastic explosives

words.definitionEn: to remove or obtain a substance from something, for example by using an industrial or a chemical process
words.exampleEn: a machine that extracts excess moisture from the air

words.definitionEn: a person whose opinions, especially about religion or politics, are extreme, and who may do things that are violent or illegal for what they believe
words.exampleEn: left-wing/right-wing/political/religious extremists

words.definitionEn: to make an action or a process possible or easier
words.exampleEn: The new trade agreement should facilitate more rapid economic growth.

words.definitionEn: a small group of people within a larger one, whose members have some different aims and beliefs to those of the larger group
words.exampleEn: There are rival factions within the administration.

words.definitionEn: a department or group of related departments in a college or university
words.exampleEn: the Faculty of Law

words.definitionEn: to become or to make something become paler or less bright
words.exampleEn: The curtains had faded in the sun.

words.definitionEn: to make bad use of something, or to use so much of something that it harms your health
words.exampleEn: to abuse alcohol/drugs

words.definitionEn: the quality of treating people equally or in a way that is reasonable
words.exampleEn: The fairness of the judicial system is being questioned.

words.definitionEn: causing or ending in death
words.exampleEn: a fatal accident/blow/illness

words.definitionEn: the things, especially bad things, that will happen or have happened to somebody/something
words.exampleEn: The fate of the three men is unknown.

words.definitionEn: a chemical element. Aluminium is a light, silver-grey metal used for making pans, etc.
words.exampleEn: aluminium saucepans/window frames

words.definitionEn: making people have a good opinion of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She made a favourable impression on his parents.

words.definitionEn: an action or a piece of work that needs skill, strength or courage
words.exampleEn: The tunnel is a remarkable feat of engineering.

words.definitionEn: doing something for pleasure or interest, not as a job
words.exampleEn: an amateur photographer

words.definitionEn: having or based on the belief that women should have the same rights and opportunities as men
words.exampleEn: feminist demands/ideas/theories

words.definitionEn: a person who supports the belief that women should have the same rights and opportunities as men
words.exampleEn: Nineteenth-century feminists demanded equal education and employment opportunities for single women.

words.definitionEn: the part of food that helps to keep a person healthy by keeping the bowels working and moving other food quickly through the body
words.exampleEn: dietary fibre

words.definitionEn: a person who takes part in a sport or other activity for pleasure, not as a job
words.exampleEn: The tournament is open to both amateurs and professionals.

words.definitionEn: angry and aggressive in a way that is frightening
words.exampleEn: a fierce dog

words.definitionEn: a device containing paper, sand, chemicals, etc. that a liquid or gas is passed through in order to remove any materials that are not wanted
words.exampleEn: an air/oil filter

words.definitionEn: to pass liquid, light, etc. through a special device, especially to remove something that is not wanted
words.exampleEn: All drinking water must be filtered.

words.definitionEn: a sum of money that must be paid as punishment for breaking a law or rule
words.exampleEn: I got a parking fine for parking on double yellow lines.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody pay money as an official punishment
words.exampleEn: fine somebody (something), The magistrate fined him $400.

words.definitionEn: an official who lives in a foreign country as the senior representative there of their own country
words.exampleEn: the British Ambassador to Italy/in Rome

words.definitionEn: a gun that can be carried
words.exampleEn: The police were issued with firearms.

words.definitionEn: a sudden attack of an illness, such as epilepsy, in which somebody becomes unconscious and their body may make violent movements
words.exampleEn: to have an epileptic fit

words.definitionEn: a sports event that has been arranged to take place on a particular date and at a particular place
words.exampleEn: There are plans to make the race an annual fixture.

words.definitionEn: a mistake in something that means that it is not correct or does not work correctly
words.exampleEn: The argument is full of fundamental flaws.

words.definitionEn: having a flaw; not perfect or correct
words.exampleEn: seriously/fundamentally/fatally flawed

words.definitionEn: to leave a person or place very quickly, especially because you are afraid of possible danger
words.exampleEn: She burst into tears and fled.

words.definitionEn: a group of military ships commanded by the same person
words.exampleEn: a fleet of destroyers

words.definitionEn: the soft substance between the skin and bones of animal or human bodies
words.exampleEn: The trap had cut deeply into the rabbit's flesh.

words.definitionEn: to change a law, document, statement, etc. slightly in order to correct a mistake or to improve it
words.exampleEn: He asked to see the amended version.

words.definitionEn: the ability to change to suit new conditions or situations
words.exampleEn: The new system offers a much greater degree of flexibility in the way work is organized.

words.definitionEn: to develop quickly and become successful or common
words.exampleEn: Few businesses are flourishing in the present economic climate.

words.definitionEn: a small change or improvement that is made to a document or proposed new law; the process of changing a document or proposed new law
words.exampleEn: to introduce/propose/table an amendment (= to suggest it)

words.definitionEn: a liquid; a substance that can flow
words.exampleEn: body fluids (= for example, blood)

words.definitionEn: in the middle of or during something, especially something that causes excitement or fear
words.exampleEn: He finished his speech amid tremendous applause.

words.definitionEn: part of a film showing a particular event
words.exampleEn: old film footage of the moon landing

words.definitionEn: a person who comes from a different country
words.exampleEn: The fact that I was a foreigner was a big disadvantage.

words.definitionEn: to put a lot of effort into making something successful or strong so that it will last
words.exampleEn: a move to forge new links between management and workers

words.definitionEn: a series of letters, numbers or symbols that represent a rule or law
words.exampleEn: This formula is used to calculate the area of a circle.

words.definitionEn: to create or prepare something carefully, giving particular attention to the details
words.exampleEn: formulate something, to formulate a policy/theory/plan/proposal

words.definitionEn: away from a place; out
words.exampleEn: They set forth at dawn.

words.definitionEn: going to happen, be published, etc. very soon
words.exampleEn: the forthcoming elections

words.definitionEn: to encourage something to develop
words.exampleEn: The club's aim is to foster better relations within the community.

words.definitionEn: a comparison of one thing with another thing that has similar features; a feature that is similar
words.exampleEn: analogy (between A and B), The teacher drew an analogy between the human heart and a pump.

words.definitionEn: easily broken or damaged
words.exampleEn: fragile china/glass/bones

words.definitionEn: formal permission given by a company to somebody who wants to sell its goods or services in a particular area; formal permission given by a government to somebody who wants to operate a public service as a business
words.exampleEn: a franchise agreement/company

words.definitionEn: in an honest and direct way that people might not like
words.exampleEn: He spoke frankly about the ordeal.

words.definitionEn: feeling annoyed and impatient because you cannot do or achieve what you want
words.exampleEn: It's very easy to get frustrated in this job.

words.definitionEn: causing you to feel annoyed and impatient because you cannot do or achieve what you want
words.exampleEn: It's frustrating to have to wait so long.

words.definitionEn: the feeling of being frustrated
words.exampleEn: in frustration, Dave thumped the table in frustration.

words.definitionEn: a heavy metal object that is attached to a rope or chain and dropped over the side of a ship or boat to keep it in one place
words.exampleEn: to drop anchor

words.definitionEn: practical and useful; with little or no decoration
words.exampleEn: Bathrooms don't have to be purely functional.

words.definitionEn: the activity of collecting money for a charity or organization, often by organizing social events or entertainments
words.exampleEn: The hospice is planning a major fundraising event for June.

words.definitionEn: a ceremony, often a religious one, for burying or cremating (= burning) a dead person
words.exampleEn: Hundreds of people attended the funeral.

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring liquid. In the UK, Canada and other countries it is equal to about 4.5 litres; in the US it is equal to about 3.8 litres. There are four quarts in a gallon.
words.exampleEn: The tankers carried 130 000 gallons of fuel.

words.definitionEn: the activity of playing games of chance for money and of betting on horses, etc.
words.exampleEn: online/internet gambling

words.definitionEn: a spirit who is believed to be a servant of God, and is sent by God to deliver a message or perform a task. Angels are often shown dressed in white, with wings.
words.exampleEn: a host of angels

words.definitionEn: a meeting of people for a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: a social/family gathering

words.definitionEn: a long, steady look at somebody/something
words.exampleEn: He met her gaze (= looked at her while she looked at him).

words.definitionEn: to look steadily at somebody/something for a long time, either because you are very interested or surprised, or because you are thinking of something else
words.exampleEn: She gazed at him in amazement.

words.definitionEn: equipment in a vehicle that changes the relation between engine speed (or pedal speed on a bicycle) and the speed of the wheels moving forwards or backwards
words.exampleEn: Careless use of the clutch may damage the gears.

words.definitionEn: a school or college for special training
words.exampleEn: She trained at the Royal Academy of Music.

words.definitionEn: shared by, including or typical of a whole group of things; not specific
words.exampleEn: ‘Vine fruit’ is the generic term for currants and raisins.

words.definitionEn: the murder of a large number of people from a particular nation or ethnic group, with the aim of destroying that nation or group
words.exampleEn: Refugees gave accounts of the mass genocide.

words.definitionEn: a quick look
words.exampleEn: to take/have a glance at the newspaper headlines

words.definitionEn: to look quickly at something/somebody
words.exampleEn: She glanced at her watch.

words.definitionEn: a sight of somebody/something for a very short time, when you do not see the person or thing completely
words.exampleEn: glimpse (of somebody/something), He caught a glimpse of her in the crowd.

words.definitionEn: deserving or bringing great success and making somebody/something famous
words.exampleEn: We congratulate you on this glorious victory.

words.definitionEn: great success that brings somebody praise and honour and makes them famous
words.exampleEn: Olympic glory in the 100 metres

words.definitionEn: the activity of governing a country or controlling a company or an organization; the way in which a country is governed or a company or institution is controlled
words.exampleEn: He emphasized the company's commitment to high standards of corporate governance.

words.definitionEn: an attractive quality of movement that is smooth and done with control; a simple and beautiful quality
words.exampleEn: She moves with the natural grace of a ballerina.

words.definitionEn: a strong hold of somebody/something or control over somebody/something
words.exampleEn: I grabbed him, but he slipped from my grasp.

words.definitionEn: to take a strong hold of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: He grasped my hand and shook it warmly.

words.definitionEn: very serious and important; giving you a reason to feel worried
words.exampleEn: The police have expressed grave concern about the missing child's safety.

words.definitionEn: a place in the ground where a dead person is buried
words.exampleEn: We visited Grandma's grave.

words.definitionEn: the force that attracts objects in space towards each other, and that on the earth pulls them towards the centre of the planet, so that things fall to the ground when they are dropped
words.exampleEn: Newton’s law of gravity

words.definitionEn: a pattern of straight lines, usually crossing each other to form squares
words.exampleEn: New York’s grid of streets

words.definitionEn: a very sad feeling, especially when somebody dies
words.exampleEn: She was overcome with grief when her husband died.

words.definitionEn: a wide smile
words.exampleEn: She gave a broad grin.

words.definitionEn: to smile widely
words.exampleEn: They grinned with delight when they heard our news.

words.definitionEn: to break or press something into very small pieces between two hard surfaces or using a special machine
words.exampleEn: to grind coffee/corn

words.definitionEn: an act of holding somebody/something tightly; a particular way of doing this
words.exampleEn: Keep a tight grip on the rope.

words.definitionEn: to hold something tightly
words.exampleEn: grip something, ‘Please don't go,’ he said, gripping her arm.

words.definitionEn: being the total amount of something before anything is taken away
words.exampleEn: gross weight (= including the container or wrapping)

words.definitionEn: to happen faster or earlier; to make something happen faster or earlier
words.exampleEn: Inflation continues to accelerate.

words.definitionEn: a member of a small group of soldiers who are not part of an official army and who fight against official soldiers, usually to try to change the government
words.exampleEn: urban guerrillas (= those who fight in towns)

words.definitionEn: help or advice that is given to somebody, especially by somebody older or with more experience
words.exampleEn: guidance for teachers on how to use video in the classroom

words.definitionEn: the unhappy feelings caused by knowing or thinking that you have done something wrong
words.exampleEn: She had feelings of guilt about leaving her children and going to work.

words.definitionEn: the tube in the body through which food passes when it leaves the stomach
words.exampleEn: It can take up to 72 hours for food to pass through the gut.

words.definitionEn: to describe somebody/something as being very good or special, especially in newspapers, etc.
words.exampleEn: be hailed (as) something, The conference was hailed as a great success.
